tools.name: profile
tools.description: Return the user's compiled personal context - a compact structured summary of who they are, synthesized from their accepted facts. No file content. Always allowed. Also reports the profile's privacy status (private, shared, network, public, or org) so you can describe it correctly.

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List the user's files (metadata only: id, name, size, content-type, modified). Always allowed.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Search the user's files by filename. Returns matching file metadata, ranked by name match. Always allowed.

tools.name: get_file
tools.description: Get the full text content of one file by id (large files are truncated; use ask_docs for targeted passages). Binary files (images etc.) return a short-lived download link instead. Audited.

tools.name: ask_docs
tools.description: Search the user's connected documents by MEANING and return the most relevant passages (the actual text), each cited with its source file, for you to read and quote. Use this to ANSWER a question from the user's documents - it returns grounded source content, not a file link, not a filename list, an

tools.name: query_table
tools.description: Run an EXACT, deterministic query over ONE tabular file (a CSV, or the first table of a spreadsheet/PDF/Word document). Use this instead of ask_docs whenever the question needs COUNTING, SUMMING, AVERAGING, MIN/MAX, FILTERING, or exact row lookups over structured data ('how many rows...', 'total amo

tools.name: suggest_update
tools.description: Suggest an update to the user's saved context (something you learned about them). It is saved as a PENDING suggestion for the user to review and accept or ignore - it does NOT directly change their profile, and nothing is ever written to their files.

tools.name: shared_context
tools.description: List and read curated personal context shared with you. With no arguments, lists who shared what (names + ids only, no content). With a `from` selector (a grantId from that list), returns that person's shared profile text. SAVED BY NAME: if the user has saved a context under a short name, pass `save

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "list_files" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "get_file" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "query_table" is 1571 chars, far longer than this server's median (266). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

findings.detail: Description of "shared_context" is 1425 chars, far longer than this server's median (266). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "shared_context" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: query_table · Private data: profile, list_files, search_files, get_file, ask_docs, query_table, suggest_update, shared_context · Public sink: query_table, shared_context · Destructive: query_table.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: query_table · Private data: profile, list_files, search_files, get_file, ask_docs, query_table, suggest_update, shared_context · Public sink: query_table, shared_context · Destructive: query_table

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "profile" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Return the user's compiled personal context — a compact structured summary of who they are, synthesized from their accepted facts. No file content. Always allowed." → after: "Return the user's compiled personal context - a compact structured summary of who they are, synthesized from their accepted facts. No file content. Always allowed. Also reports the profile's privacy status (private, shared, network, public, or org) so you can describe it correctly."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
